insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to utilize the new @inContext directive with the channelId argument. This will enable them to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, providing more accurate and relevant results for merchants. When migrating, developers should ensure that their app handles cases where the channelId is not provided or is invalid. They should also review the Storefront API documentation for any additional requirements or restrictions on using the @inContext directive with the channelId argument.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and relevant data being fetched for their sales channels, leading to a better overall experience. This change will also enable developers to build more customized and sales-channel-specific features for merchants.
insight.why_it_matters: This update matters because it provides developers with more flexibility and control over the data they fetch from the Storefront API, allowing them to build more customized and sales-channel-specific features for merchants. It also enables merchants to have more accurate and relevant data for their sales channels.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various objects and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, making it easier for app developers to build merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for your app to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing apps to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.

recap.headline: Shopify updates enhance event triggers, Liquid templates, and app analytics

themes.title: Event Triggers and Webhooks
themes.detail: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for Order, Collection, InventoryItem, and more. This update expands the capabilities of Webhooks in the GraphQL Admin API.

themes.title: Theme Development and App Analytics
themes.detail: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building. Additionally, full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, reducing the need for external libraries and data warehouses.

themes.title: Inventory Management and Security
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview introduces new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API. Furthermore, the card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, enhancing security for payment processing.

actions.text: Update apps to use new event triggers and metafield changes
Test physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Implement mTLS certificate for card deposit endpoint
